records.ety: [OE. wischen, weschen, wuschen, AS. w<?/scan; akin to D. wenschen, G. wünschen, Icel. æeskja, Dan. önske, Sw. önska; from AS. w<?/sc a wish; akin to OD. & G. wunsch, OHG. wunsc, Icel. <?/sk, Skr. vā<?/chā a wish, vā<?/ch to wish; also to Skr. van to like, to wish. <?/. See Winsome, Win, v. t., and cf. Wistful.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Wished (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Wishing.]

defs: To have a desire or yearning; to long; to hanker.
quotes: They cast four anchors out of the stern, and wished for the day.
This is as good an argument as an antiquary could wish for.

defs: To desire; to long for; to hanker after; to have a mind or disposition toward.
quotes: I would not wish Any companion in the world but you.
I wish above all things that thou mayest prosper.

defs: To frame or express desires concerning; to invoke in favor of, or against, any one; to attribute, or cal down, in desire; to invoke; to imprecate.
quotes: I would not wish them to a fairer death.
I wish it may not prove some ominous foretoken of misfortune to have met with such a miser as I am.
Let them be driven backward, and put to shame, that wish me evil.

defs: To recommend; to seek confidence or favor in behalf of.
quotes: I would be glad to thrive, sir, And I was wished to your worship by a gentleman.
Behold, I am according to thy wish in God a stead.

defs: Expression of desire; request; petition; hence, invocation or imprecation.
quotes: Blistered be thy tongue for such a wish.

defs: A thing desired; an object of desire.
quotes: Will he, wise, let loose at once his ire . . . To give his enemies their wish!
